source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An adventurous archaeologist crosses between two rock cliffs by slowly going hand over hand along a rope stretched between the cliffs. He stops to rest at the middle of the rope as shown in figure. The rope will break if the tension in it exceeds \( 2.50 \times 10^4 \) N, and our hero’s mass is 90.0 kg. What is the smallest value \( \theta \) can have if the rope is not to break?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the figure is central
question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
the angle θ and rope geometry appear to be defined by the diagram rather than fully specified in text
multi_step: solving requires combining the mass/weight with the rope tension limit and geometric angle relation
the setup implies at least a force-balance step plus solving for the minimum allowable angle
multiple-choice numeric options suggest a calculational physics problem rather than direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a specific numeric result and unit-like angle notation
the problem has a standard physics constraint formulation, so the final value can be checked by equation-based verification
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support reliable downstream checking

risk_flags: angle orientation is diagram-defined and may be ambiguous without the image

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantic assets are complete
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high image dependency is adequately supported because the referenced figure exists
multi-step and verifiability scores are strong, indicating good candidate quality for downstream use
the noted ambiguity is diagram-related, but the diagram is available, so it is not a blocking risk
